question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged external user flood the network with transactions to create a dense record_file table such that the synchronized prune() method holds the importer thread lock indefinitely during mass deletion, blocking the importer from writing new consensus data and causing a total network shutdown?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a scenario where the prune() method deletes record_file rows that the importer's stream file parser still references as its last-processed position, causing the importer to lose its place in the stream and halt ingestion of new transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: hasNext()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction pattern that results in a record_file with a consensusEnd value such that the arithmetic expression current.getConsensusEnd() + batchPeriod overflows a long, causing hasNext() to loop infinitely and permanently blocking the importer scheduler thread?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: prune(RecordFileIterator)] Can an unprivileged external user cause the transactionOperations.executeWithoutResult() block inside prune(RecordFileIterator) to hold a long-duration exclusive database lock on transaction tables, blocking the importer from inserting newly confirmed transactions and causing a network-wide confirmation halt?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: hasNext()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the reference equality check current == max in hasNext() — where max is a JPA-managed entity that may be a different object instance than current even when representing the same record file — to cause an infinite loop that permanently stalls the importer?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged external user submit transactions that cause the retention period (retentionProperties.getPeriod().toNanos()) to produce a negative offset when passed to findLatestWithOffset(), resulting in the query selecting the most recent record file as the pruning boundary and deleting all current transaction data, shutting down the network?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that prune() is annotated @Scheduled with fixedDelayString derived from retentionProperties.getFrequency().toMillis() to cause the scheduler to re-enter prune() immediately after completion, creating a continuous deletion loop that starves the importer of database write capacity?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: prune(RecordFileIterator)] Can an unprivileged external user cause a repository.prune(endTimestamp) call inside the transactionOperations.executeWithoutResult() lambda to throw a runtime exception that is caught by the outer try-catch in prune(), leaving the database in a partially pruned state where the importer cannot determine its correct stream position and halts?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: hasNext()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a burst of transactions that creates record files with consensusEnd values spaced exactly at batchPeriod boundaries, causing findNextBetween() to be called millions of times per prune() invocation, exhausting database connection pool resources and blocking new transaction writes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that RecordFileRepository.prune() deletes record_file rows with consensusEnd <= endTimestamp, causing the importer's stream file downloader — which relies on the latest record_file entry to determine what to download next — to lose its anchor and stop downloading new stream files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the synchronized prune() method to deadlock with another synchronized importer component that also acquires the same object monitor, permanently blocking both threads and halting all transaction ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: hasNext()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a record_file insertion pattern where findNextBetween(endTimestamp, max.getConsensusEnd()) always returns a non-empty result just below max, causing hasNext() to never set current = max and loop indefinitely, blocking the importer scheduler?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: prune(RecordFileIterator)] Can an unprivileged external user cause the counters.merge(table, count, Long::sum) call to overflow a Long value when pruning a very large dataset, producing a corrupted counter state that causes subsequent prune iterations to behave incorrectly and delete active transaction data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the initialDelay = 120_000 ms window before the first prune() execution to flood the database with record files that will all be pruned in the first run, causing a massive single-transaction deletion that locks all transaction tables and prevents new transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getTableName()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a RetentionRepository implementation to be injected whose getClass().getInterfaces()[0] returns an unexpected interface, causing getTableName() to produce an incorrect table name that bypasses shouldPrune() filtering and deletes a critical system table needed for transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged external user submit transactions that manipulate the max(consensus_end) value in the record_file table such that findLatestWithOffset() returns a record file with a consensusEnd equal to the most recent active record file, causing prune() to delete all current transaction data and halt the network?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: hasNext()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that batchPeriod is read from retentionProperties.getBatchPeriod().toNanos() on every hasNext() call — making it susceptible to a race condition if the property is updated mid-iteration — to cause the iterator to skip or re-process record files and delete active transaction data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: prune(RecordFileIterator)] Can an unprivileged external user cause the retentionRepositories.forEach() loop inside transactionOperations.executeWithoutResult() to fail partway through — after deleting transactions but before deleting record_file entries — leaving the database in an inconsistent state where the importer cannot resume stream processing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the prune() method catches all Exception types and only logs them, meaning a critical database failure during pruning is silently swallowed, leaving the importer in an unknown state that prevents new transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: hasNext()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the findNextBetween(0, max.getConsensusEnd()) call in the initial hasNext() invocation to return a record file with consensusEnd = 0, causing subsequent arithmetic in the iterator to produce a negative endTimestamp that triggers deletion of all records with consensusEnd <= 0, corrupting the database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a minimum retention period validation in RetentionProperties to configure period = Duration.ZERO, causing findLatestWithOffset(0) to return the second-most-recent record file and prune all but the very latest transaction, effectively halting historical transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: prune(RecordFileIterator)] Can an unprivileged external user cause a database connection timeout during the transactionOperations.executeWithoutResult() block to leave an uncommitted transaction that holds row-level locks on the transaction table, blocking the importer from inserting new confirmed transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the synchronized keyword on prune() only prevents concurrent execution within the same JVM instance, allowing multiple importer instances in a clustered deployment to simultaneously run prune() and cause conflicting deletions that corrupt the record_file table?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: hasNext()] Can an unprivileged external user submit transactions that create a record_file gap (no record files between two timestamps) such that findNextBetween() returns empty mid-iteration, causing hasNext() to set current = null and terminate pruning prematurely, leaving stale data that confuses the importer's stream position tracking?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that retentionProperties.getPeriod().toNanos() can silently overflow for very large Duration values (> ~292 years in nanoseconds), causing findLatestWithOffset() to receive a negative offset and select the most recent record file as the pruning boundary?
